rag_rules: Place input assumptions and output guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use only visible feature names in component type contracts; avoid subcomponent paths
Write output dependency guarantees as relations between visible outputs and inputs
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts in component types
Declare helper variables like `value_in_range` with explicit types matching their expressions
generic_rules: Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over non-numeric data types
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Align helper declaration types with the expression types they represent
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq` for equations and `guarantee` for guarantees
Boolean expressions should evaluate to bool type
Integer arithmetic should use int type
Real arithmetic should use real type
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not encode AADL property names as AGREE variables
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
